title: Dasystephana decumbens

intro: Dasystephana decumbens is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the "decumbent dasystephana" or "small purple daisy." The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Dasystephana decumbens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
